CP00-264 (Risk C, 150 descriptor-only units) holds up under source inspection. The pack honestly claims `production_ready: false` / "implemented_pending_review" and makes no premature production or enterprise-trust claim. I verified:
- Pack binding, phase/micro/deliverable counts all reconcile to 150 and are computed from real units, not hardcoded.
- All 7 required exports exist, are wired through `index.js`, deep-equaled into the contract, and asserted by both the package test and the contract validator.
- Descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries are enforced in code and rejected on drift by the validator.
- Authority boundary intact: Claude read-only, not final approver; human approval required; Hermes is evidence-only, not product runtime.
- Review-hardening blockers cover every rejection class in the prompt; all 19 gate commands recorded at exit 0; auth preflight passed.

**Findings:** 1× P3 (non-blocking) — `changed-file-scope.json` lists `new-session-handoff.md` in both the pack-implementation and unrelated-preserved buckets. Cosmetic classification ambiguity in the tooling artifact; no impact on boundaries or the readiness claim. The pack-level claim is honestly scoped: production_ready=false, status "implemented_pending_review"/"implementation_and_plan_gates_passed_pending_final_closeout" — it makes NO premature production or enterprise-trust claim. Source inspection (Read/Grep/Glob) confirms the claim is supported: (1) SEARCH_CORE_CP264_PACK_BINDING matches the baseline exactly (risk C, 150 units, range, upstream CP00-263, next CP00-265, next_subphase RP07.P08.M02.S15); (2) phase counts P07:106+P08:44=150, micro-phase/micro-title row counts, and the 7-deliverable distribution all reconcile and are computed from real included_units (validators.js:6030 countBy), not hardcoded; (3) all 7 required public exports exist (service.js + validators.js), are surfaced via index.js export *, deep-equaled into contracts/search-core-contract.json, and asserted by both the package test (model.test.js:1876-1927) and the contract validator (validate-rp07-search-core-contract.mjs:1428-1456), with valid===true derived from real error arrays; (4) descriptor-only/no-write/no-real-data boundaries enforced — freezeCp264Result (service.js:3888) pins ~40 runtime/write flags false and no_real_data/synthetic_only true, and the foundation validator rejects rows that flip descriptor_only/runtime_execution/customer_safe/raw_payload or the cross-tenant/lock/retry/blocked-claim/Hermes/permission/audit/no-real-data/Claude/human guards; (5) authority boundary honored — claude_is_final_approval false, human_final_approval_required true, no enterprise-trust-from-local-validation, no_write_attestation.promotes_claude_to_final_approval false; (6) Hermes patterns are descriptor/evidence only (emits_hermes_runtime_receipt false, executes_command_runtime false), not product runtime; (7) review-hardening forbidden_review_evidence covers auth failures, zero-byte output, malformed/fenced JSON, tool-call-shaped output, disabled tools, missing raw-output/source-inspection references, and Claude-final-approval/source-mutation/enterprise-trust claims. Command-evidence reports all 19 gate commands at exit 0; readiness-report shows auth preflight passed and ready=true. No TODO/FIXME/stub/throw markers in the search src; all upstream references (CP263 builders, searchCoreRowKey, freezeCp264Validation) resolve.
